analytical_depth.rationale: Exceptional understanding of IndAS 115 accounting impact on real estate valuation, correctly dismissing distorted PE metrics. Minor gap: could have explored the specific margin profile of the upcoming delivery cluster.

logical_consistency.rationale: Multiple inconsistencies in final calculations (e.g., MoS uses 2437 instead of calculated 2409; Gordon model uses 20% ROE in text but 15% in JSON).

completeness.rationale: Thorough coverage of valuation dimensions and mandatory metrics. Minor gap: lacks a deeper dive into the specific geographic mix of the land bank driving the NAV premium.

actionability.rationale: Provides a clear, corrected valuation signal with well-defined scenarios. Minor gap: the bear case triggers are somewhat generic and could be tied more closely to specific project delays.

sector_framework.rationale: Flawless application of real estate frameworks, focusing on presales backlog and completion accounting. Minor gap: could have explicitly modeled the cash flow timing of the ₹20,000 Cr annual land spend.

data_sourcing.rationale: Claims are well-backed by cited data. Minor gap: relies heavily on management guidance for future ROE without independently verifying the margin expansion path.

tool_use_discipline.rationale: Good fallback logic (using reverse DCF when DCF was empty), but made manual variable substitution errors in the calculate calls and required an excessive number of tool searches.

cost_efficiency.rationale: Cost of $1.03 and 41 turns significantly exceed the acceptable thresholds (>$1.00 and >20 turns), indicating highly inefficient tool planning.

issues.issue: The blended base fair value calculation used 2190 for consensus instead of the stated 2207, and the subsequent Margin of Safety calculation used a hallucinated 2437 instead of the calculated 2409.
issues.suggestion: Ensure variables passed to the calculate tool exactly match the figures stated in the text.

issues.issue: The Justified P/Adj Book calculation in the text uses a 20% ROE (yielding 1.28x), but the calculate tool was called with 15% ROE, and the JSON summary reports the 15% ROE result (0.57x).
issues.suggestion: Maintain consistency between the assumptions stated in the narrative and those passed to the calculator/summary.

strengths: Masterful handling of the IndAS 115 completion accounting distortion, correctly overriding the tool's automated PE-based signals.
Excellent sector-specific analysis of the presales-to-revenue bridge, quantifying the unrecognized earnings locked in the backlog.
real_estate.summary: This is an analytically brilliant report that perfectly captures the nuances of Indian real estate valuation under IndAS 115. However, it suffers from poor cost efficiency (41 turns) and several sloppy variable substitution errors in its final fair value and MoS calculations.
